key_insight: A perfectly matched layer can suppress upper-boundary wave reflections in compressible Boussinesq models with much less damping thickness than traditional sponge layers.
engineering_relevance: This offers a more compact and potentially more effective boundary treatment for atmospheric dynamical cores that must absorb acoustic and gravity waves near the model top.
method: Derive PML formulations for both linear and nonlinear compressible Boussinesq equations.
Use a novel nonlinear PML that damps perturbations about a hydrostatically balanced reference state.
Discretize the PML with a compatible finite element method and test against sponge-layer baselines.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments showing the PML outperforms a typical sponge layer for acoustic-wave absorption and removes standing-wave reflections from orographic gravity waves.
watch_item: The approach is demonstrated in idealized Boussinesq tests, so robustness, parameter tuning, and cost in full atmospheric models still need validation.
paper_title: A perfectly matched layer for damping vertically propagating waves in the compressible Boussinesq equations
